title: carmichaelia petriei
intro: Carmichaelia petriei, also known as the Petrie's broom, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant is native to New Zealand and has been used by the indigenous Māori people for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Carmichaelia petriei include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of this plant can be made into a tea, infusion, or poultice, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
